content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 30, 1998

Gregory W. Britain
Attorney At Law
Great Western Plaza, Suite 300
330 North "D" Street
San Bernardino, California 92401

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-98-115

Dear Mr. Britain:

This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your question implicates issues that have already
occurred. Please note that the Commission does not give advice regarding past conduct.
(Regulation 18329.)

You represent Chuck Dunn in a conflict of interest case against the Jurupa Unified School
District ("District") and a former board member, David Barnes. Mr. Dunn was a teacher with the
District. You allege that Mr. Barnes had a conflict of interest in voting on Mr. Dunn's dismissal
from the District because Mr. Dunn was attempting to purchase the local newspaper where
Mr. Barnes worked. Mr. Dunn would have replaced Mr. Barnes as editor of the newspaper.

QUESTION

Is a tort claim under Government Code Section 905 required to be brought for an action
under Section 91005(b)?

CONCLUSION

The Commission implements and interprets the Political Reform Act. Government Code
Section 905 is outside of the Act. Therefore, we cannot advise you regarding Government Code

_______________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-98-115
Page 2

Section 905. We can tell you that the Act does not require you to file a tort claim under
Government Code Section 905 before you may file a claim pursuant to any section of the Act.

QUESTION

Must the bond required in Section 91012 include costs for attorney's fees?

CONCLUSION

Section 91012 states:

"The court may award to a plaintiff or defendant other than an
agency, who prevails in any action authorized by this title his costs
of litigation, including reasonable attorney's fees. On motion of
any party, a court shall require a private plaintiff to post a bond in a
reasonable amount at any stage of the litigation to guarantee
payment of costs."

Pursuant to the second sentence of Section 91012, the court must require a private
plaintiff to post a bond in any "reasonable amount" to guarantee payment of "costs." It is clear
from the first sentence of Section 91012 that "costs" in this context may include attorney's fees.
It is also clear that the court has wide discretion in determining the amount of the bond.

QUESTION

Is there a private right of action for a violation of the conflict of interest laws?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Section 91005(b) states:

"(b) Any designated employee or public official specified in
Section 87200, other than an elected state officer, who realizes an
economic benefit as a result of a violation of Section 87100 or of a
disqualification provision of a conflict of interest code is liable in a
civil action brought by the civil prosecutor or by a person residing
within the jurisdiction for an amount up to three times the value of
the benefit."

Please see Chapter 11 of the Act (Sections 91000-91015) for other pertinent enforcement
provisions.

File No. 1-98-115
Page 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By:    Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
